text: And we'll do something that is somewhat unusual for a routine lobectomy in that we will use some muscle from his chest wall to reinforce or buttress the bronchial closure because of his underlying pulmonary issues.
And as we talked about before starting the case, because of the infectious issues in his chest, we were planning to harvest a piece of muscle to help reinforce the closure of the airway.
And that's what we're going to do now.
And so now we're scoring the center of each rib, which will allow us to harvest the periosteum off the rib.
